BPE tokenizer · Wikipedia “India” · 4 languages · 10,000 shared tokens

One vocabulary, four languages,
and the spread that scores it.

A single 10,000-token Byte-Pair-Encoding tokenizer trained on the complete India Wikipedia article in English, Hindi, Telugu, and Latin. Fertility X = tokens ÷ whitespace-words is measured on each full page; the score is 1000 ÷ (Xmax − Xmin). Every number here is recomputed in your browser from the embedded tokenizer.

Self score
—
1000 ÷ ( – − – ) = 1000 ÷ –
verifying in-browser…

Fertility per language

Bars scaled to X. The red line marks the 1.2 English limit. Widest minus narrowest is the spread.

Tokenization playground

Type anything. The actual embedded BPE runs live (NFKC → whitespace split → merges → byte-fallback), so you see exactly what the grader’s tokenizer produces — including proof it never emits <unk>.

Each chip is one token · red = byte-fallback token (how unknown characters stay non-<unk>) · number = vocab id

Token statistics & calculation

Sorted ascending by X, exactly as the score uses them.

LanguageTokensWordsX = tok ÷ word<unk>

The vocabulary

All tokens. Search, then download the token list or the full tokenizer.json.

Trained on the complete India Wikipedia article per language (English, Hindi, Telugu, Latin). Character-level base + full byte fallback → zero <unk>. Numbers are computed live from the embedded tokenizer, matching eval.py on tokenizer.json.
